name: Daemon Commit Logic

tasks.title: Refactor autoCommitPartialWork into a configurable daemon commit function
tasks.description: Refactor autoCommitPartialWork into a configurable daemon commit function
tasks.body: The current autoCommitPartialWork (iteration.go:549) is hardcoded for the failure path. Refactor it into a function that accepts a commit reason (success or failure), an attempt number (for failure messages), and the full GitConfig. The function should: (1) Check auto_commit is enabled. (2) Check the path-specific flag (commit_on_success or commit_on_failure). (3) Stage changes: git add -u for code changes; git add .wolfcastle/ only if commit_state is true. (4) Use the spec's commit message format: success = 'wolfcastle: <task-id> complete', failure = 'wolfcastle: <task-id> partial (attempt <N>)'. (5) Respect skip_hooks. The existing autoCommitPartialWork call site (iteration.go:384) should use the refactored function.

tasks.title: Add success-path commit to iteration loop
tasks.description: Add success-path commit to iteration loop
tasks.body: In the success path of RunOnce (around iteration.go:358-373, after state propagation), call the refactored commit function with reason=success. This is the counterpart to the failure-path call at line 384. The commit should happen after all state mutations (task completion, parent propagation) are done, so the commit captures the final state. If commit_on_success is false, skip it. Wire the Daemon's Config.Git through to the call.

tasks.title: Implement staging area preservation
tasks.body: The current autoCommitPartialWork runs 'git add -u' and 'git add .wolfcastle/' which can clobber the user's manually staged changes. Implement staging area preservation per the spec. The preferred approach: use a temporary GIT_INDEX_FILE for daemon commits, leaving the user's index untouched. The alternative (stash-based) approach is: (1) git stash --keep-index --quiet, (2) git add ..., (3) git commit, (4) git stash pop --quiet. The spec notes the stash approach may be fragile; prefer the separate index approach if feasible. Whichever approach is chosen, document the decision in an ADR via 'wolfcastle adr create'.

tasks.title: Add tests for daemon commit logic
tasks.description: Add tests for daemon commit logic
tasks.body: Write tests covering: (1) Success-path commit produces correct message format ('wolfcastle: <task-id> complete'). (2) Failure-path commit produces correct message format with attempt number. (3) No commit when auto_commit is false. (4) No commit on success when commit_on_success is false. (5) No commit on failure when commit_on_failure is false. (6) .wolfcastle/ excluded from staging when commit_state is false. (7) Staging area preservation: user's manually staged changes survive a daemon commit. (8) Clean working tree produces no commit (no empty commits). (9) --no-verify passed when skip_hooks is true. Use the testutil environment builder pattern from existing tests.

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Daemon Commit Logic is complete and correct

audit_enrichment: Verify staging area preservation: create a file, git add it manually, run daemon commit, confirm the file remains staged in the user's index
Verify no empty commits are produced when the working tree is clean
